description: Generate an editable Figma Slides deck for Clearfolio KRW 2B sale-readiness buyer diligence. The deck must be sober, enterprise, evidence-led, and explicit about claim boundaries. It should use existing repo evidence rather than marketing promises: buyer-demo upload/viewer flow, seeded demo screenshots, KPI evidence, data-room manifest, readiness scorecard, license/SBOM clearance, security and durability gaps, and no library/submodule split decision. Figma Code Connect must not be used.
objectives: Give enterprise buyers a concise diligence walkthrough of what Clearfolio can inspect today.
Show Product Design, Data Analytics, Figma, Ponytail, and engineering evidence in one buyer-readable story.
Keep partial gates visible as discount risks instead of styling them as complete.
State that KRW 2B is a sale-readiness target, not a valuation opinion.

content: Clearfolio Buyer Demo Diligence Pack
KRW 2B sale-readiness evidence package
Not a valuation opinion; repository and design evidence as of 2026-07-03
outline.purpose: Set the buyer-review context and claim boundary.
outline.subject: Clearfolio Buyer Demo Diligence Pack
visuals: Small status strip: buyer-demo evidence, diligence package, claim boundary

content: 1. What can be inspected today
2. Buyer demo journey
3. KPI and readiness evidence
4. Security, license, and integration posture
5. Remaining discount risks and next closure order
outline.purpose: Preview the evidence sequence a buyer can inspect.
outline.subject: Buyer Review Path
visuals: Numbered agenda list with ready/partial markers

content: Buyer-demo shell: upload, KPI strip, history, viewer handoff
Seeded local story: success, processing, unsupported format, dead-letter
Evidence panels: KPI snapshot exports and operator recovery posture
outline.purpose: Show the product surface that exists now.
outline.subject: Inspectable Product Evidence
visuals: Three evidence blocks with ready labels

content: Open root shell
Load demo story or upload document
Inspect status/history and job details
Open viewer and artifact path
Review KPI and recovery evidence
outline.purpose: Explain the end-to-end flow without raw implementation detail.
outline.subject: Buyer Demo Journey
visuals: Left-to-right flow from upload to governed preview

content: Runtime jobs: seeded demo shows 4 jobs; pilot target 50K monthly documents
Ready previews: seeded demo shows 1 ready preview
Successful preview rate: seeded demo mixed-state 50 percent; supported pilot target 99.5 percent
P95 preview latency: seeded demo 4200 ms; small pilot target under 10 seconds
KPI export: 1 tenant-scoped local evidence export
outline.purpose: Tie buyer KPIs to current proof and target thresholds.
outline.subject: Data Analytics Evidence
visuals: Metric table with current proof and threshold columns

content: 23 data-room artifacts: 15 ready, 8 partial, 0 external
8 readiness gates: 3 ready, 5 partial, 0 external
Conservative gate readiness: 38 percent
Partial gates: design handoff, data analytics KPI, security compliance, buyer integration, production durability
outline.purpose: Quantify current data-room readiness while keeping gaps visible.
outline.subject: Buyer Readiness Scorecard
visuals: Ready/partial rollup with amber discount-risk list

content: Product demo: seeded story, FigJam board, QA evidence
License attribution: SBOM, license review, third-party attribution
Readiness scorecard: manifest, generated scorecard, diligence index
Mandatory local gates: compile, test, JaCoCo, JavaDoc, Markdown lint, Semgrep
outline.purpose: Summarize the strongest current diligence claims.
outline.subject: Ready Evidence Gates
visuals: Four proof cards with green ready styling

content: Design handoff: high-fidelity frames still incomplete
Data analytics KPI: durable projections and pilot volume data pending
Security compliance: production OIDC/JWT, role mapping, durable audit pending
Production durability: SQL jobs, artifact metadata, revocation, read audit, metrics store pending
outline.purpose: Prevent overclaiming and focus the closure roadmap.
outline.subject: Remaining Discount Risks
visuals: Amber risk cards with next closure cues

content: Keep boundaries in-repo now: one real runtime and no second production consumer
No submodule or SDK package yet: it adds diligence friction without current buyer value
Split later only when converter adapters, analytics contracts, or client SDKs need independent versioning
outline.purpose: Document the Ponytail no-split decision for buyer diligence.
outline.subject: Architecture Packaging Decision

content: Keep license, attribution, manifest, and scorecard gates green
Generate and attach buyer diligence Slides deck and high-fidelity frames
Import-test connector seed in buyer-like tenant
Replace gateway HMAC scaffold with validated OIDC/JWT and role mapping
Promote ledgers and lifecycle evidence into durable stores
outline.purpose: Show the shortest path from current evidence to stronger buyer-release readiness.
outline.subject: Next Closure Order

content: KRW 2B is a sale-readiness target, not a valuation opinion
Seeded demo proof is local evidence, not production data
Queued GitHub checks and review process are not implementation blockers
Primary artifacts: PR #82, FigJam board, data-room manifest, readiness scorecard
outline.purpose: End with a precise buyer-facing boundary.
outline.subject: Claim Boundary
visuals: Boundary statement and link placeholders for repo, PR, FigJam

rules: Do not use Figma Code Connect. Do not imply a completed acquisition valuation. Use ready/partial language consistently. Keep slides dense but readable. Avoid decorative marketing hero visuals; use proof tables, diagrams, and concise captions. Mark seeded demo evidence as local demo proof, not production data.

theme.paletteDescription: Quiet enterprise palette: white background, charcoal text, blue primary, green ready evidence, amber partial risk, red blocked or claim-boundary warnings.

title: Clearfolio Buyer Demo Diligence Pack
userIntent: Create a buyer diligence Slides deck for the Clearfolio KRW 2B sale-readiness program and make it editable in Figma Slides.
